recipes.title: Wiener Schnitzel
recipes.description: Crisp golden Viennese veal cutlet pounded thin and fried in lard or butter, served with lemon and parsley potatoes.
steps: Pound each veal cutlet between two sheets of cling film to 4 mm thickness.
Season the cutlets with salt and pepper.
Dredge each cutlet first in flour, then dip in beaten egg, and coat lightly in breadcrumbs without pressing.
Heat the clarified butter or lard in a wide pan to about 170°C until it pops when a crumb is dropped in.
Fry one cutlet at a time, swirling the pan so the schnitzel swims in fat, for about 90 seconds per side until deep golden.
Lift onto a paper towel to drain and keep warm while you fry the rest.
Serve immediately with a wedge of lemon and chopped parsley.

recipes.title: Tafelspitz
recipes.description: Austrian boiled beef in a clear root-vegetable broth, traditionally served with apple-horseradish and chive sauce.
steps: Char the cut side of the onion in a dry pan until blackened, for color and flavor.
Place the beef and bones in a pot, cover with cold water, bring to a boil and skim well.
Add the charred onion, salt and peppercorns and simmer very gently for 2 hours.
Add the carrots, celeriac, leek and bay leaves and cook for another 45 minutes until the beef is fork tender.
Lift the beef out, slice across the grain, and arrange in warm soup plates.
Strain the broth and ladle some over the meat with diced root vegetables.
Mix the grated apple and horseradish for the apfelkren sauce, and serve alongside with chives.

recipes.title: Kaiserschmarrn
recipes.description: Austrian shredded fluffy pancake dusted with powdered sugar and served with plum compote, supposedly Emperor Franz Joseph's favourite.
steps: Soak the raisins in the rum for 15 minutes.
Whisk the egg yolks with half of the sugar and the vanilla until pale.
Stir in the flour, milk and salt to a smooth batter.
Whip the egg whites with the remaining sugar to stiff peaks and fold into the batter with the raisins.
Melt half the butter in a wide skillet and pour in the batter.
Cook over medium heat for 4 minutes until the underside is golden, then flip in pieces using two spoons.
Add the remaining butter, tear the pancake into rough chunks, and continue cooking until everything is golden.
Dust generously with powdered sugar and serve hot with plum compote.

recipes.title: Sachertorte
recipes.description: Famous Viennese dense chocolate cake with apricot jam and a glossy dark chocolate glaze, served with whipped cream.
steps: Preheat the oven to 170°C and line a 24 cm springform with parchment.
Melt the chocolate and let cool slightly. Cream the butter and powdered sugar, then beat in the egg yolks and melted chocolate.
Whip the egg whites with the sugar and salt to stiff peaks. Fold a third into the chocolate mixture, then fold in the rest with the sifted flour.
Pour into the tin and bake for 50 minutes until a skewer comes out with moist crumbs. Cool fully and slice horizontally.
Warm the apricot jam, sieve it, and spread on the bottom layer; sandwich the cake back together and brush the outside with more jam.
Boil the glaze sugar and water for 5 minutes to a syrup, remove from heat and stir in the chopped chocolate until smooth.
Pour the glaze over the cake in one go and spread to a glossy coating with a palette knife. Let set without disturbing.
Slice and serve each piece with a generous spoon of unsweetened whipped cream.

recipes.title: Apfelstrudel
recipes.description: Austrian flaky strudel pastry filled with cinnamon-spiced apples, raisins and toasted breadcrumbs.
steps: Mix the flour, water, oil, yolk, salt and vinegar to a soft dough, knead until silky for 10 minutes, oil lightly and rest covered for 1 hour.
Soak the raisins in the rum for 30 minutes.
Toss the apple slices with sugar and cinnamon. Toast the breadcrumbs in a little butter until golden.
Spread a clean cloth on the table, dust with flour, and stretch the dough out from the center until paper-thin and translucent.
Brush the dough with melted butter, scatter breadcrumbs over two thirds, then arrange apples and raisins on top.
Use the cloth to roll up the strudel tightly, tuck in the ends, and place seam-down on a buttered tray.
Brush all over with melted butter and bake at 190°C for 35 to 40 minutes until deep golden, basting once.
Rest briefly, dust with powdered sugar and slice. Serve warm with cream or vanilla sauce.

recipes.title: Beuschel
recipes.description: Austrian creamy ragout of veal lung and heart in a sour cream and lemon sauce, traditionally served with bread dumplings.
steps: Place the lung and heart in a pot with the diced vegetables, bay leaves, peppercorns and salt and cover with water.
Simmer gently for 90 minutes until very tender, then lift out and let cool. Strain and reserve 600 ml of the broth.
Slice the cooled offal into thin strips, removing any sinews.
Melt the butter in a saucepan, stir in the flour and cook to a light blond roux.
Whisk in the reserved broth and simmer to a smooth sauce.
Stir in the vinegar, mustard, lemon zest, anchovy paste and the sliced offal and warm for 10 minutes.
Off the heat stir in the sour cream and parsley and adjust seasoning.
Serve hot with bread dumplings or crusty bread.

recipes.title: Linzer Torte
recipes.description: Austrian shortcrust torte from Linz, the world's oldest known cake, with redcurrant jam under a lattice top.
steps: Combine the flour, ground hazelnuts, sugar, cinnamon, cloves, lemon zest and salt in a bowl.
Rub in the cold butter until the mixture resembles fine crumbs.
Stir in the egg yolks and a splash of egg white to bring the dough together; wrap and chill for at least 2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 180°C and butter a 24 cm tart tin.
Press two thirds of the dough into the tin and up the sides, then spread with the redcurrant jam.
Roll the remaining dough into long ropes and arrange in a lattice over the jam, sticking the ends to the rim.
Brush the lattice with egg wash and bake for 35 to 40 minutes until deep golden.
Cool fully in the tin before slicing.

recipes.title: Frittatensuppe
recipes.description: Austrian beef broth garnished with thinly sliced strips of savory pancake, a comforting classic.
steps: Whisk the flour, milk, eggs, salt and nutmeg into a smooth thin pancake batter and rest for 15 minutes.
Heat a small skillet, melt a little butter, and pour in just enough batter to coat thinly.
Cook each pancake for 1 minute per side until lightly golden and stack on a plate.
Roll each cooled pancake tightly and slice into 5 mm wide strips.
Bring the beef broth to a gentle simmer and adjust the salt.
Divide the pancake strips between warm bowls and ladle the hot broth over them.
Sprinkle with plenty of chives and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Käsespätzle
recipes.description: Tyrolean soft egg noodles layered with grated mountain cheese and caramelized onions, baked until bubbly.
steps: Whisk the flour, eggs, milk, salt and nutmeg into a thick, sticky batter and rest for 15 minutes.
Slowly fry the sliced onions in butter over low heat for 25 minutes until deep golden and crisp.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Press the batter through a spätzle maker or a colander into the boiling water.
Cook for 2 minutes after the spätzle float, then lift out into a buttered baking dish.
Layer the spätzle with the grated cheeses and a quarter of the onions, ending with cheese on top.
Bake at 200°C for 10 to 15 minutes until melted and bubbly.
Top with the remaining caramelized onions, chives and black pepper and serve hot.
